text: ‘A bloodless offering’; literally, in the Latin, ‘an offering (by way) of sacrifice’, in the Hebrew text simply, ‘a gift’. The word is technically used to distinguish offerings made of vegetable things from those which involved the destroying of animal life.
Literally, ‘as a memorial’. The use of the word in this connexion is obscure, but it is always used of these (less expensive) vegetable offerings.
